title: T-041 — Production outbox dispatcher and D365 WO confirmation anti-corruption adapter
prompt:
# T-041 — Production outbox dispatcher and D365 WO confirmation anti-corruption adapter

PRD/UX refs: §8.2.7, §11.7, UX PROD-012

Goal: Implement async outbox dispatcher mapping completed WO events to D365 JournalLines without making D365 a release or completion source-of-truth.

Implementation details:
Use production_outbox_events from T-009 and d365_push_dlq from T-010. Create a mapper from canonical WO/output/consumption/waste rows into D365 JournalLines payload. Dispatcher respects integration.d365.push.enabled and dry_run settings, retries with exponential backoff, and moves exhausted failures to DLQ. D365 success/failure updates outbox delivery status only; it must never mutate factory release status, WO START eligibility, active_bom_header_id, or active_factory_spec_id.

Scope files:
- packages/api-services/production/d365-outbox-dispatcher.ts [create]
- packages/api-services/production/d365-journallines-adapter.ts [create]
- apps/web/app/api/internal/jobs/production/d365-dispatch/route.ts [create]

Acceptance criteria:
1. Completed WO event maps to D365 JournalLines and marks outbox delivered on adapter success
2. Adapter failure after max attempts creates d365_push_dlq with raw canonical and mapped payloads
3. D365 delivery state never changes release_status or active BOM/spec fields

Evidence: include screenshots/artifacts and Playwright traces/artifacts for UI/E2E tasks; include API test output for backend tasks.

risk_red_lines: Do not treat D365 sync/export/Built as production release source-of-truth
